title: Meals
description: Learn meals in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to Meals
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign meals in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 4 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Breakfast'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Breakfast'. The first meal of the day. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'B' handshape taps mouth/chin.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Lunch'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Lunch'. A meal eaten in the middle of the day. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'L' handshape taps mouth/chin.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Snack'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Snack'. A small amount of food eaten between meals. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'K' handshape taps mouth/chin.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Dinner'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Dinner'. The main meal of the day, eaten in the evening. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'D' handshape taps mouth/chin.

pages.title: Practice: Breakfast
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Breakfast'. The first meal of the day.

How to sign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'B' handshape taps mouth/chin.

pages.title: Practice: Snack
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Snack'. A small amount of food eaten between meals.

How to sign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'K' handshape taps mouth/chin.
